nodes.context: Governing body of organized crime in New York City, consisting of Five Families.
Bootlegging and racketeering gang led by Dutch Schultz and Joey Noe in the Bronx.
Irish-American organized crime group in New York, led by Jack 'Legs' Diamond.
Jewish-American organized crime network, including figures like Arnold Rothstein.
Splinter group formed by Vincent Coll after leaving Schultz's gang, aiming to take over Schultz's territory.
Confederation of mobsters across the United States, coordinating organized crime activities.
Enforcement arm of the National Crime Syndicate, responsible for contract killings.
Dutch Schultz's criminal organization involved in bootlegging and numbers rackets in New York City.
The governing body of the American Mafia in New York City, which ordered Schultz's murder.
An Irish-American criminal organization based in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan.

edges.context: Schultz's gang rivaled the Italian crime syndicates that became the Mafia's Five Families.
Bootleg war between Schultz's gang and Jack Diamond's Irish mob over territory in Manhattan.
Vincent Coll formed his own crew to murder Schultz and take over his territory, leading to a gang war.
Schultz was a member of the National Crime Syndicate, a confederation of mobsters.
Murder, Inc. was the enforcement arm of the National Crime Syndicate.
The Commission (American Mafia) was part of the National Crime Syndicate.
Murder, Inc. acted as the enforcement arm for the Five Families.
The Five Families ordered Schultz's murder after he disobeyed their orders.
Murder, Inc. hitmen killed Schultz and his associates on orders of the Five Families.
John M. Dunn, an Irish mobster associated with Schultz, survived the purge and later became a powerful member of the Hell's Kitchen Irish mob.
